title: Gaussian Free Field in beta ensembles and random surfaces
speaker: Jeremy Quastel

proof_stages.label: KPZ equation and lateral-growth heuristic
proof_stages.summary: The speaker introduces the one-dimensional KPZ height equation, explains its heat, noise, and nonlinear lateral-growth terms, and relates it formally to stochastic Burgers.

proof_stages.label: Microscopic KPZ-class growth and ASEP height functions
proof_stages.summary: Discrete growth models are presented as microscopic KPZ-class objects, with ASEP singled out as a tunable particle/height model whose flat initial condition will later be analyzed.

proof_stages.label: Hopf-Cole interpretation and competing KPZ scalings
proof_stages.summary: Directed-polymer partition functions motivate the stochastic heat equation and Hopf-Cole solution of KPZ, after which the speaker distinguishes weakly asymmetric diffusive convergence to KPZ from true long-time KPZ fixed-point scaling.

proof_stages.label: Initial-data subuniversality and exact-formula landscape
proof_stages.summary: The talk organizes KPZ fluctuations by initial data, recalls Tracy-Widom and later exact formulas for special data, and identifies flat/point-to-line GOE-type cases as a major missing nondeterminantal regime.

proof_stages.label: Replica delta-Bose-gas heuristic
proof_stages.summary: Moments of the stochastic heat equation are shown to solve the attractive delta Bose gas, and the physicists’ divergent replica series and Airy/Mellin-Barnes regularizations are explained as the heuristic source of exact KPZ formulas.

proof_stages.label: Fredholm Pfaffian formula for flat ASEP
proof_stages.summary: The new result is stated: a tau-deformed exponential transform of the flat ASEP height function has an exact Fredholm Pfaffian formula, with the special symmetric q-exponential and Pfaffian kernel reflecting flat ASEP symmetries.

proof_stages.label: Duality-based construction and half-flat generating functions
proof_stages.summary: The proof route is outlined: Borodin-Corwin-Sasamoto ASEP duality gives a discrete delta-Bose-gas problem, a guessed Bethe-ansatz-style moment formula handles half-flat data, and summing these moments yields convergent generating functions leading to GOE and flat KPZ limits.

examples.name: Eden growth model
examples.note: The lattice cluster grown by randomly adding boundary boxes illustrates KPZ-class one-dimensional interfaces and the dependence of limiting fluctuations on curved versus flat initial geometry.
examples.engagement_reason: the speaker spells out the Eden growth rule step by step and reasons through its large-scale shape and fluctuation scaling

examples.name: Directed random polymer partition function
examples.note: The polymer partition function visibly produces the key Hopf-Cole idea, since it satisfies a discrete stochastic heat equation and its logarithm is therefore KPZ-like.
examples.engagement_reason: the speaker constructs the directed polymer energy and partition function and explains how it satisfies a discrete stochastic heat equation whose log is KPZ-like

examples.name: Last-passage percolation limit
